response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Thanks so much Shane, I’ve booked us in for next week. Excited to chat. Have a great rest of the week and weekend. Warm Regards Heidi   From: Shane Buckley <shane@portless.com> Sent: Friday, 5 September 2025 12:10 AM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Sagitine - top level info   Hey Heidi,    Thanks for the info. Some interesting stuff here certainly. Some size products would be better suited to other products. Could we have a call whenever it suits you?    There are a few things I can go through from your list that would be easier to chat through. Is this possible from your end?   Book a meeting with me   Thanks Shane      On Tue, Sep 2, 2025 at 7:31 AM Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > wrote: Hi Shane, How are you.  Thanks so much for your patience with me.   Here are a few details to get the ball rolling.   Sagitine is a luxury brand specializing in premium storage solutions. Our products are designed for customers who value craftsmanship, longevity, and elegance, and we have established a strong market presence in Australia but are yet to expand into any other market. We sell storage boxes as well as a Stand which is effectively a racking system for the boxes - https://sagitine.com/boxes When consumers purchase a stand the colour of the boxes they choose are included - https://sagitine.com/stands   I’m preparing to launch into the Asian market early in 2026, and have been considering warehousing facilities on the east coast of China. As part of this expansion, we are looking for a warehouse partner who can support us in two key areas:   1. Asian Market Fulfillment 2. Warehousing with the aim that some of the product would be on-shipped to Australia.   I have two key manufacturers that are both based in China – one for the stand and one for the boxes. I’m currently shipping the orders separately to Australia and holding them in warehousing facilities here. However, I’d like to hold stock in a Chinese warehouse and then send combined orders (both Boxes and Stands) in a single container to Australia.   In terms of the Asian Market Fulfillment, I am still exploring where I’ll look to expand into. Location of warehousing facilities will have a bearing on market.   Below are the details of the main products we sell.   All Stands have several cartons. For example, if a customer purchases the New York 20 Box Stand they would receive 4 cartons. 1. New York Frame 2. 2 x Milan Shoe Box 6 pack 3. 1 x Milan Shoe Box 8 pack At the moment we don’t require any pick’n pack facilities.  The goods go directly to the customer as they were received from the manufacturer.   That’s probably a good starting point for now.  Let me know if there are other key things I can send through. And then I’ll look to lock in another meeting.   Thanks again Shane Cheers Heidi     From: Shane Buckley < shane@portless.com > Sent: Tuesday, 19 August 2025 7:36 PM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Subject: Re: Heidi Melville booked a meeting with: Shane Buckley   Sorry we missed each other.    Please do give some top level points and I will answer via email. We have had several Australian brands come aboard in the past few weeks all Craft Club Referrals and they are seeing cost and expansion benefits   Thanks Shane     On Tue, Aug 19, 2025 at 9:45 AM Shane Buckley < shane@portless.com > wrote: Hey Heidi,    So Sorry about jumping on the wrong zoom link earlier.  I am on our meeting link now and I will stay on it for the next 15 minutes if you see this email and have a chance to jump back on.    Thanks Shane    On Tue, Aug 19, 2025 at 9:40 AM Shane Buckley < shane@portless.com > wrote: Hey Heidi,    Looks like I hopped on the wrong Zoom link. So sorry about that. Are you free to jump back on now? Thanks and Kind Regards  Shane Buckley  Portless Growth Consultant  Book a meeting with me     On Tue 19 Aug 2025 at 09:38, Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > wrote: Hi Shane, I jumped on the call but it looks like you’ve been caught up this morning. If you’re weather is anything like Sydney I get it – it’s torrential here!   I might flick you through an email with the top line points on it and see if we can get some info exchanged over email first, and then try to jump on a call. Hope you have a great day Cheers Heidi x   From: Shane Buckley < shane@portless.com > Sent: Friday, 15 August 2025 6:19 AM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Subject: Heidi Melville booked a meeting with: Shane Buckley     New Meeting Booked with Shane Buckley     Email address heidi@sagitine.com Date / time 19 August 2025 18:30 AEST (UTC +10:00) Location https://portless.zoom.us/j/86252059960?pwd=VtCkHocmo9eAPj8NT5FOfrndn0BYc9.1     Note: if you need to make changes to your meeting, you can here: Reschedule Cancel           -- Thanks and Kind Regards  Shane Buckley  Portless Growth Consultant  Book a meeting with me   -- Thanks and Kind Regards  Shane Buckley  Portless Growth Consultant  Book a meeting with me   -- Thanks and Kind Regards  Shane Buckley  Portless Growth Consultant  Book a meeting with me

subject: RE: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures
customer_question: How do I return this?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Morning Ladies and welcome back Jayne! I hope you had the best time.  Cannot wait to hear all about it.   Thanks a mill for the below numbers and for changing the meeting to Thursday. Re the pick-ups in Bris, I feel like I’ve pestered the customers a bit now, so will just wait to hear back from them and if I don’t that’s all good.   And no worries not to have the Meta numbers until today but looking forward to seeing them. Jayne, just for some more context I’m trying to work out how we missed our April target. * Compared to last year we were completely stocked – last year we ran out of all Olive on day 1 and New York stands in the first week. * We ended up increasing spend significantly. * Our influencers all fired off really well. * And we had great new imagery and vids for ads.   The two key metrics I can see in Shopify are: AOV is down -   525 in 2025 versus 670 in 2024 And Returning customers were up heavily – 39% in 2025 versus 26% in 2024   AOV we know is driven by the inserts, but the returning customer rate is really interesting.  It’s great for brand credibility but does it mean we didn’t play hard enough in prior prospecting or top of funnel? Be interesting to look into.   Let me know what you think. Hides x   From: Jayne Coulston <paid@decantagency.com.au> Sent: Monday, 19 May 2025 8:51 AM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Cc: Kelly Decant <info@decantagency.com.au> Subject: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures   Good morning!!   I am back and ready to go! Hope you're well, I see your call was moved to Thursday, so we will catch up on everything then, but let me know if there is anything in particular that you need in the meantime. I will get you those numbers you asked for as well today  Here are details from the last 7 days of performance:   LAST 7 DAYS  Shopify: Total sales - $3,934 Sessions - 953 Conversion rate - 0.31% Total orders - 3 AOV - $1,158.79 RCR - 0%   Meta: Spend - $556.67 Website Visits - 851 CTR (click-through-rate) - 1.82% Purchases - 1 ATC's - 1 ROAS - 3.05   Google: Impressions - 10,845 Spend - $220.47 Clicks - 109 Purchases - 1 (valued at $1,685.01) CTR - 1.01%     PRIOR 7 DAYS  Shopify: Total sales - $892.36 Sessions - 1,186 Conversion rate - 0.17% Total orders - 3 AOV - $252.12 RCR - 66.67%   Meta: Spend - $673.99 Website Visits - 859 CTR (click-through-rate) - 0.68% Purchases - 3 ATC's - 3 ROAS - 1.61   Google: Impressions - 12,701 Spend - $242.70 Clicks - 145 Purchases - 0 (valued at $0) CTR - 1.14%   Thanks Hides

subject: RE: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures
customer_question: How do I assemble this?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Morning Kell,   Sorry I didn’t get to this yesterday.   Logo attached but only in Black. We don’t have brand guideline for logo usage – because the only people that normally do creative are Zach and myself. But I’m sure the team will do great!   Let me know if you want to run anything past me before you send it live, but also no need to if you’re happy with it.   And Insta login is: Acc: info@sagitine.com Pss: 2025e&c!TeD   Yay!  Thanks again Kel xx     From: Kelly Campbell <info@decantagency.com.au> Sent: Wednesday, 23 April 2025 3:03 PM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures   Hey Heidi, Are you able to send across your logo files and brand guidelines please? I would like to pop the small white version of the logo if you have one, tastefully at the bottom centre of the Chloe video in your BA ad, as it gives some context while they're putting it together. And don't forget to shoot across your IG login when you have a moment so I can keep an eye on how tagged posts affect your web traffic from IG + profile traffic     Kindest, Kell   On Tue, 22 Apr 2025 at 10:02, Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > wrote: No worries at all Kell x   From: Kelly Campbell < info@decantagency.com.au > Sent: Tuesday, 22 April 2025 10:00 AM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Subject: Re: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures   5 mins away sorry Heidi   On Tue, 22 Apr 2025 at 07:38, Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > wrote: Thanks Kel, Let’s do 10 today but 11 from the next one.   Anyone that comes through Gifted we can use without asking, we own the content. Any collabs we organised outside of Gifted that are paid normally have a 12 month licensing. Anyone like Chloe we ask for the content and if we can use it.  She’s normally a super star and happy to share and happy for us to use. Honestly, it’s the smaller content creators that are the hardest.  The bigger well established people are often very supportive.   Chat soon Hides x   From: Kelly Campbell < info@decantagency.com.au > Sent: Tuesday, 22 April 2025 7:33 AM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Cc: Jayne Coulston < paid@decantagency.com.au > Subject: Re: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures   I've got an 11 booked, so let's go 10AM, but I can move you back to 11AM from next fortnight if that's a better timeslot?    No stress re pickups, he can also do tomorrow morning or afternoon now too.   I've got answers to most of those Qs already prepped to talk through, but I'll have any additional notes ready for our call 👌🏼 One quick one though, do we have approval to use any/all of our paid collabs in ads, or do we ask non-organised customers to use their content in ads, like Chloe?     Kindest, Kell   On Tue, 22 Apr 2025 at 07:18, Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > wrote: Morning Kell,   Thanks a mill for the numbers. Yup, def great to chat this morning. I’ve got it in the diary for 10am but it’s normally at 11. Let me know what time you’ve got and what works best for you, both are good for me. And no word from the pick-ups!  Sorry, I’ll follow them up again this morning.   Below are a few notes on how the Warehouse Sale is tracking and what’s coming up this week.   We’ve got just over a week to go and are tracking at 57% to target, which I’m really happy with.   We had a cracking start and a slow middle but have a few pieces of activity to help us pick up at the end.   SOCIAL: Last night, a Sagitine Fave, Chloe posted some unscheduled content from a few purchases she made and gave us a huge boost. https://www.instagram.com/chloemorello/   And we still have a few Influencers yet to post: * Thanee Cook 220K https://www.instagram.com/itstahnee/ * Phoebe Spiller 170k https://www.instagram.com/phoebespiller/ * Meg Saunders 17K https://www.instagram.com/missmeganjadee * Sim Spy Style 13K https://www.instagram.com/sim.spys.stylist * Summah 12K https://www.instagram.com/_summahpayne   I’ll also up the organic posting and story frequency and post daily now until the end of the campaign.   EMAILS: We start to pick up email frequency for the final week of the campaign too. I trialled an email yesterday arvo, takes 24 for full stats to load but it’s not tracking as a ‘best performer’.     PAID PERFORMANCE * How did the update to the brand awareness campaign perform?  We included a sale message. * You good for message change out on Thurs to FINAL DAYS and then Weds to FINAL HOURS? * I’ll let the pricing run through Thursday May 1 st as well, just to make sure anyone who’s late to the party has a chance to convert. * Let me know what our best performing creative is and what we have scheduled for May1st and if we need anything new. * And finally, any opportunity to dial anything up to ensure we hit or overachieve on the target of $90k?   Chat later this morning Hides x     From: Kelly Campbell < info@decantagency.com.au > Sent: Tuesday, 22 April 2025 6:45 AM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Cc: Jayne Coulston < paid@decantagency.com.au > Subject: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures   Hey Heidi, Hope you and the family had a lovely long weekend break. Any word back from your Brisbane customers on pickup?   Here are details from the last 7 days of performance:   LAST 7 DAYS (Your Usual Mon - Sun) Shopify: Total sales -$8,808 Sessions - 3,730 Conversion rate - 0.54% Total orders - 22 AOV - $323.76 RCR - 35%   Meta: Spend - $3,872.56 Website Visits - 1,613 CTR (click-through-rate) - 1.09% Purchases - 18 ATC's - 39 ROAS - 1.93   Google: Impressions - 23,949 Spend - $845.26 Clicks - 251 Purchases - 4 (valued at $2,056.47) CTR - 1.05%     PRIOR 7 DAYS  Shopify: Total sales - $6,234 Sessions - 2,938 Conversion rate - 0.48% Total orders - 15 AOV - $374.49 RCR - 60%   Meta: Spend - $3,990.94 Website Visits - 1,613 CTR (click-through-rate) - 1.01% Purchases - 11 ATC's - 37 ROAS - 1.34   Google: Impressions - 22,570 Spend - $654.54 Clicks - 246 Purchases - 3 (valued at $2,217) CTR - 1.09%   Are we okay to double up and still chat this morning? It would be good to run through notes with you to make sure we're across everything! And no word from the pick-ups!  Sorry, I’ll follow them up again this morning.   Below are a few notes on how the Warehouse Sale is tracking and what’s coming up this week.   We’ve got just over a week to go and are tracking at 57% to target, which I’m really happy with.   We had a cracking start and a slow middle but have a few pieces of activity to help us pick up at the end.   SOCIAL: Last night, a Sagitine Fave, Chloe posted some unscheduled content from a few purchases she made and gave us a huge boost. https://www.instagram.com/chloemorello/   And we still have a few Influencers yet to post: * Thanee Cook 220K https://www.instagram.com/itstahnee/ * Phoebe Spiller 170k https://www.instagram.com/phoebespiller/ * Meg Saunders 17K https://www.instagram.com/missmeganjadee * Sim Spy Style 13K https://www.instagram.com/sim.spys.stylist * Summah 12K https://www.instagram.com/_summahpayne   I’ll also up the organic posting and story frequency and post daily now until the end of the campaign.   EMAILS: We start to pick up email frequency for the final week of the campaign too. I trialled an email yesterday arvo, takes 24 for full stats to load but it’s not tracking as a ‘best performer’.     PAID PERFORMANCE * How did the update to the brand awareness campaign perform?  We included a sale message. * You good for message change out on Thurs to FINAL DAYS and then Weds to FINAL HOURS? * I’ll let the pricing run through Thursday May 1 st as well, just to make sure anyone who’s late to the party has a chance to convert. * Let me know what our best performing creative is and what we have scheduled for May1st and if we need anything new. * And finally, any opportunity to dial anything up to ensure we hit or overachieve on the target of $90k?   Chat later this morning Hides x     From: Kelly Campbell <info@decantagency.com.au> Sent: Tuesday, 22 April 2025 6:45 AM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Cc: Jayne Coulston <paid@decantagency.com.au> Subject: Sagitine - Weekly Ad Figures   Hey Heidi, Hope you and the family had a lovely long weekend break. It seems that even when I save it, Meta overrides it.      Let me know if you have any questions at all and if you wanted to call before I leave (next Monday, 14th is my last day)

subject: RE: Sagitine 2025 order prep
customer_question: My item arrived damaged
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} That’s amazing!   Can we make it 1.30pm on Wednesday (Sep 3).   I’ll bring in a few different examples of the Nougat boxes so you can see how they’ve faded in different light. See you next week Heidi x   From: Sheree Camus <sheree@nextpack.com.au> Sent: Tuesday, 26 August 2025 3:24 PM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Cc: Stewart Melville <Stewart@sagitine.com> Subject: RE: Sagitine 2025 order prep   Hi Heidi,   You must be psychic.   I literally thought of you this morning and wondered how business is.  I feel like this isn’t the first time you’ve popped into my mind and then an email comes though!!!   Not surprised things are doing well.  Fabulous product and excellent marketing!!!     Let me know when suits you to come in.  Monday, Wednesday or Thursday next week works.  Any time after 11:30am.  Do any of these days work for you?     Look forward to catching up and going through all that is listed below. Pls bring in Nougat with the green hue.   We can certainly emboss onto the leather.   Let’s nut everything out when you come in.     Best Regards   Sheree Camus Founder & Creative Director Nextpack Pty Ltd       M 0413 289 065 E    sheree@nextpack.com.au W nextpack.com.au T   02 9145 8620 A  Suite 302, 115 Military Road Neutral Bay NSW 2089 Australia P  PO BOX 753 Neutral Bay NSW 2089 Australia         From: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Sent: Tuesday, 26 August 2025 3:01 PM To: Sheree Camus < sheree@nextpack.com.au > Cc: Stewart Melville < Stewart@sagitine.com > Subject: Sagitine 2025 order prep   Hi Sheree, How are you? I hope you and the team are all great and soaking up this sunshine.   We’ve had another cracker 12 months so I’m in the process of preparing the details for our next big order.    I appreciate where we are with timing and that we won’t have stock in warehouse for November, but to give you an idea of where I’m at, I’m hoping to be able to place the order with you in September. I’m just juggling some cash flow issues, so as soon as I have that sorted, I’ll be able to lock it in.   In the meantime, there are are a few things and new ideas I’d love to chat with you about and thought it might be worth popping in next week or the week after.   1. Nougat – I now have a few examples of the boxes going green hued – would love to show you so we can work out if we can solve the issue. 2. The Inserts went so well.  Would love to sample a few other types.  Make-up, letter writing, and wine bottles. 3. Is it possible to emboss something on a small sample of the Leather Pulls before they’re attached to the Boxes?  I’m keen to try a Luna New Year Limited Edition Cherry Box with a Horse Motif on the Leather Pull. 4. We’ll sample 4 new colours again. Still working on the Pantone’s but the colours we’re looking at are – Burgundy, Mustard, Forest Green and Peach. 5. I’d also love to trial a lidded box that sits inside the Milan Shoe Box as a GWP.  We trialled a GWP of the Insert Sets in June as an offer and it went so well.   Let me know when is a good time to pop in. Looking forward to catching up. Cheers Heidi     Heidi Melville Director SAGITINE M: 0478 401 914 E:  heidi@sagitine.com

response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Good Morning Yvette, Thank you so much for the below. That would be very very good if we can have the order ready to ship on January 19 th .  Thank you for making that work. I will not send an inspector this time. Can you please send photos of the quality for inspection with specific attention to the following for inspection: * Detailed photos of the Timber Top. * The quality of the welding on the corner * The quality of the powder coating * Ensuring all of the side rails are attached properly.   For the goods going to the China warehouse.  I have attached the shipmarks. These will need to be attached to each carton, you will see that there is a shipmark per product. They don’t need to be attached permanently, just so that the warehouse can scan them in, but they will then remove them before they go to the customer.   I appreciate you trying for a further discount on the order.   I hope you have a lovely rest of the week and please let me know if there are any issues or concerns with the shipment, and if there are any further delays. I will be changing the artwork for the ‘hardware’ box this time to include a QR code, can that too please be printed directly onto hardware carton?   I have also attached the damage goods ledger from the last order to date.   Here is a summary. My ultimate aim is to reach a a defective stock rate of 2%, however, for the next order I am happy to aim for under 3%. Let me know your thoughts. (I have attached the full ledger with images).   I will organise the labels and barcodes in the coming days so they are ready as well.   Please let me know if there’s anything else you need. Warm Regards Heidi x   Heidi Melville Director SAGITINE M: 0478 401 914 E:  heidi@sagitine.com       乾淨無病毒。 www.avast.com

subject: RE: Sagitine YTD down 20% rev
customer_question: Where is my order?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Sooo lovely to catch-up yesterday Jayno, Sounds like you had the absolute best time OS.    When you get a sec the follow-ups from yesterday were; * An email summary on the Meta changes from April, and if possible, the date that the changes happened. * Did you have any other comparable clients that struggled in April? * Is there any quantifiable indication that the long weekends and the election had an impact. * Let’s change our May Ads to all Advantage Plus and then check in next call to see how they’re going. * And can I please get the same Meta numbers you sent by month for J-A 2025 for the whole of 2024.   With our annual rev down 20% and ad spend up 30% we spoke about how I need to find some costs savings until I can work out a plan to get our run rate back up. I think I’ll run with your suggestion of pausing Google for a moment please.   I want to continue to run the current Perf Max campaign until the end of the month – you know how I love clean monthly results tracking. But I’m really familiar with Google so can watch that myself.   Therefore, can we please pause Google Account Management Fees from today with no need for you to do anything in the account.   I will take a look at our overall marketing plan and target and budget forecast and come back to you early next week with a relook at the numbers and activity for the remainder of 2025.   Thanks a mill Jayen Hides x       From: Jayne Coulston <paid@decantagency.com.au> Sent: Wednesday, 21 May 2025 11:50 AM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Sagitine YTD down 20% rev   No worries at all!   I have already been getting my notes together, and am looking into everything. To be completely honest, there aren't any huge reg flags, but I will share my thoughts with you tomorrow.

response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Good Morning Zev,   I hope you’re having a great week. And my apologies I haven’t got back to you sooner.   I would love to connect.  How does early next week work for you?  Either Monday or Tuesday. In the meantime I’ll get you the weight/dims of our product range. Thank you again for following up. And really looking forward to chatting. Warm Regards Heidi   From: Zev Spiro <zev@portless.com> Sent: Tuesday, 22 July 2025 5:38 AM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Sagitine <> Portless   Hey Heidi,    Hope you enjoyed the weekend. Wanted to make sure you received my last email and see when it would make sense for us to connect and discuss further.    Thank you! Zev   On Tue, Jul 15, 2025 at 8:34 AM Zev Spiro < zev@portless.com > wrote: Hey Heidi,    Thank you for all that information. My family and I just moved houses last week, so when my wife sees your products I know she's going to be all over that :-)   We have two facilities in Shenzhen and two in Vietnam, but we ship all over the world. Regarding charges, we have a very transparent pricing model with just three charges: (1) shipping, cost depends on product weight/dims and destination, (2) pick/pack, and (3) storage.    Happy to discuss all of this and delve in further on a call, as I'd love to learn more about your business and understand where we can unlock value. If you are able to provide weight/dims of your core products and average order volume per month in advance of a call it could be helpful.    Let me know your availability, or feel free to schedule some time directly  with my calendar .   .    Looking forward to it!   Zev   On Mon, Jul 14, 2025 at 10:08 PM Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > wrote: Hi Zev,   My apologies I haven’t got back to you sooner. Thank you so much for reaching back out.   I own and run Sagitine ( www.sagitine.com ).  We’re a luxury storage brand the specialises in non-plastic storage boxes for shoes and other luxury accessories. At the moment we only distribute through Australia but I’m looking into launching into Asia next year and have through about having a Chinese 3PL partner to help facilitate the expansion.   I’d love to get an idea of where in China you operate from and also what your fees and charges are. Is there any introductory information you need from me as a starting point? Thank you again Heidi     From: Zev Spiro < zev@portless.com > Sent: Tuesday, 15 July 2025 9:14 AM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Cc: Nikos Maniaty < nikos@craftclubco.com > Subject: Re: Sagitine <> Portless   Hi Heidi,    Circling back on this introduction from Nikos in case you missed it. I'd love to connect, learn more about your brand, and explore if Portless could support it. Let me know when is a convenient time for you to connect, or feel free to schedule directly with the calendar link I previously provided.    Thank you! Zev   On Thu, Jul 3, 2025 at 10:02 AM Zev Spiro < zev@portless.com > wrote: Thank you Nikos, I really appreciate the introduction!   Nice to meet you, Heidi. I'd be happy to connect and learn more about your brand and explore if there is a fit with Portless. Let me know when would be a convenient time for you, or feel free to find a good time with my calendar .    Looking forward to the conversation! Zev   On Thu, Jul 3, 2025 at 12:00 AM Nikos Maniaty < nikos@craftclubco.com > wrote: Hey Zev,    Just wanted to introduce you to Heidi from Sagitine who has some more questions about Portless for you!    Will leave it to you two to connect.

subject: RE: Screw missing
customer_question: My item arrived damaged
response_body: Hi Pallavi, Thank you so much for your message, and I’m really sorry to hear that one of the screws was missing from your hardware pack. You’re absolutely right to expect everything to be there, especially for a piece like the Shanghai Stand, and I completely understand your disappointment. Thank you as well for stepping in and using your own screw so you could finish assembling it, I really appreciate you letting me know. To properly make this right, I’d love to send you a little something special. We’re about to launch a new piece next month called the Sagitine Treasure Chest, and I’d love to send one to you as a gift ahead of the release as a thank you for your patience and understanding. I’ll arrange that to go out to you as soon as they arrive. Thank you again for your support of Sagitine, Pallavi. It truly means a lot. Warm regards, Heidi x. -----Original Message----- From: Pallavi Kumar <pallavi.kumar08@gmail.com> Sent: Thursday, 5 March 2026 7:34 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Screw missing Hello, I just recently purchased a Shanghai stand from you, order #3487. One of the screws was completely missing, and I had to replace it with one of my own screws, see photo. Given the premium quality of your product and brand, and how much these products cost, I am throughly disappointed. Pallavi Kumar

subject: RE: Shanghai Stand
customer_question: When will my order ship?
response_body: Good Morning Susan, Thank you for reaching out and for your lovely words about our collection — it’s always a pleasure to hear from someone who shares our appreciation for beautiful storage. To answer your questions: Our New York Stand is only available in its current configuration, and we’re not able to offer a bespoke or expanded version for three rows of Milan Boxes. It's something we've tried before and the tricky part is producing a one off size of the Walnut Timber Top that fits, looks stunning and has the product integrity. At present, we don’t offer a stand specifically for the Miami Boxes, but they do stack beautifully. The New York Stand in Off White has just arrived back in stock — you can view it here - https://sagitine.com/new-york-stand/new-york-20-box-stand-white-with-cream-leather-handles One elegant option some other clients have done is to place two stands side-by-side, which can create a beautifully symmetrical look while maximising storage capacity. I've measured it up and unfortunately it won't fit into the max 85cm space you have but could be an option there were other places in your home you were considering. I hope that helps and please let me know if there's anything else I can help with. Warm Regards Heidi x -----Original Message----- From: Susan Allin <susanallin@mac.com> Sent: Monday, 11 August 2025 7:22 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Shanghai Stand Hi Sagitine Team, I am looking for a new solution for shoe storage and saw your gorgeous stands and boxes. I have a couple of questions: 1) is there any possibility of a wider stand to fit three rows of Milan boxes? Ie the New York stand, with three rows of boxes (30), rather than two (20)? Is it possible to purchase extra parts to expand the stand? The area that I have can accommodate a width between 73-85cm, so looking to optimise the number of shoes I can store. 2) do you offer stands for the Miami boxes? 3) Will you restock the off white New York stand? Many

customer_question: How do I assemble this?

Please use it at check out for 20% off your first order.  I can only be used once and will expire at 9am on Saturday morning. It’s not available in conjunction with any other offers.   I hope you have a wonderful rest of the week. Warm Regards Heidi x   From: Susan Allin <susanallin@mac.com> Sent: Wednesday, 13 August 2025 5:48 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Cc: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Shanghai Stand   Hi Heidi,   Thanks very much for your email!   Unfortunately the only other space I have for shoes is 83cm wide, so not wide enough for two racks sadly (but wide enough for two rows of Miami boxes).   I will see how I go with the single New York stand :)   I had a 20% off code for first order emailed to me that unfortunately seems to have expired now. Is there any possibility of extending this for a day or two please so I can process the order? The code was  M7NLL475.   Many

subject: RE: Shipping
customer_question: Where is my order?
response_body: Good Morning Cheryl, Thank you so much for your message. I hope you had a lovely weekend. Shipping to Adelaide for the Florence 8-Box Stand is $55, and it will automatically appear in your cart once you enter your address. Just a quick note on how our Boxes and Stands work together; they’re designed as a storage piece, so the Boxes slide neatly into the Stand, much like placing a box onto a shelf. There’s no drawer mechanism that connects them to the frame. Each Box has a purposefully snug fit to help keep out household dust and protect your treasures. The result is a piece that’s as beautiful as it is practical. Our Stands are a stunningly elegant way to store, organise, and protect the things you love. Please let me know if there's anything else I can help you with. Warm regards, Heidi x -----Original Message----- From: Cheryl <cheryltrianta@gmail.com> Sent: Monday, 27 October 2025 8:32 AM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Shipping How much is shipping to Adelaide for the eight drawer unit please?

The shipping cost is a flat fee of $50.   The height of the 20-Box Stand is 130cm (52 cm wide and 34cm deep) – you can find all other dimensions and information on our website here . response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Hello Robert,   Thank you so much for the below. That’s all great news. Initially I think we would have about a 20CBM container load of product delivered in about December. Regarding timeframe, I’m looking for a long term partner that will help us to warehouse and grow over the coming years.   For courier access, we would use a company like DHl, FEDEX or UPS. The requirement would be for the goods to be labelled and ready to be collected and somewhere for the courier to access to pick up the goods.   If you could please let me know what information you need from me to be able to quote on the below, that would be great.   1. Inbound devanning (RMB / container) 2. Palletising (pallet + wrap + labour, RMB / pallet) 3. Storage (RMB / pallet / week and / month) 4. Outbound handling (RMB / pallet or / waybill for courier handover) 5. Ancillaries: receiving outside normal hours; insurance; bonded vs non-bonded; photo-on-receipt (optional) 6. Minimums and contract term ; start-up/one-off fees   Thank you again for your email and look forward to hearing back from you Warm Regards Heidi x   From: Power Forwarding Ltd <info@powerforwarding.com> Sent: Wednesday, 15 October 2025 7:43 PM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: RE: Storage-only Warehouse in/near Dongguan - Palletise + Courier Pickup (Sagitine)   Dear Heidi,   My agent in China advises as below:   Yes, we do provide exactly the services you're looking for. Our warehouse facility offers: ·         Storage-only solutions  with flexible terms ·         Palletised storage  with professional handling ·         Courier collection facility  - you can arrange your own couriers for local deliveries ·         Inventory management support  if needed We have experience working with international brands and understand the requirements for products transitioning from manufacturing to global markets. To provide you with more specific information about pricing and capacity, could you share: ·         The approximate volume of goods you plan to store (pallet quantity or CBM) ·         Your expected storage timeframe ·         Any specific requirements for the courier access We'd be happy to arrange a call to discuss your needs in more detail, or provide a customized quotation once we have these basic details. Looking forward to hearing from you.     Thanks   Kind Regards   Robert Power   Power Forwarding Ltd   Tel: 0845 094 6082   Amber House Chapel Lane Amber Hill Boston Lincolnshire PE20 3RJ   See our video below!   Registered in England & Wales No. 08789984   VAT Registration Number 112 5184 51   Quotations are valid for 30 days only and exclude duty / VAT unless specified otherwise.   All business is undertaken in accordance with the latest edition of the British International Freight Association (BIFA) Standard Trading Conditions which limit the liability of the Company,   a copy will be supplied on request.   This message is private and confidential. If you have received this message in error, please notify us and remove it from your system.   Any disclosure, copying or distribution by you is prohibited and may be unlawful. Disclosure to any party other than the addressee, whether inadvertent or otherwise is not intended to waive privilege or confidentiality. subject: RE: Sydney Click & Collect?
customer_question: What's the status of my pre-order?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Oh Pooi Nie, I hope you’re okay.  Migraines are the absolute worst.   I’ll pop my advice in dot points so it’s easier to digest for you x   Availability: * Black Shanghai 14-Box Stand - In Stock * Off-White Shanghai 14-Box Stand - On pre-order with an estimated delivery of February. * Miami Boxes in Off-White and Black - In Stock   Wear: Both the Black and Off-White wear really well.  I have the Black that I’ve used pretty much daily for five years, and it still looks as good as new.    Styling Advice: TLDR; * Black Shanghai for bedroom * Black Miami for in cupboards * Off-White Shanghai for downstairs   The Black Shanghai would look amazing in your bedroom. Absolutely think that’s a great choice. It will work so well with the darkened pallet and will add to the drama but will be so chic.    I get the perfectionism!  Me too, so I would match the colour of the pieces in the room, so if you go with the Black Shanghai in your bedroom, do Black Miami’s for in your wardrobe.  But, I don’t think you need to match the colours across the house.  You could definitely do Off-White if you wanted to add a Stand downstairs.   I have the Black in my bedroom and then the Deep Blue in my office and am so happy that they match the environment they’re in rather than matching each other.   I hope that helped! Please let me know if there’s anything else I can with. And good luck with the migraine.  I hope you’re resting and it’s passed by the time you read this. Warm Regards Heidi x   From: Pooi Nie Liew <pooinie@gmail.com> Sent: Thursday, 27 November 2025 10:20 AM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Sydney Click & Collect?   Oh gosh Heidi,   Thanks so much - I really appreciate your kindness!   Hoping for some help with my indecisiveness, if I could ask your opinion? My bedroom is quite dark (deliberately for my migraine condition): brown/black floorboards, antique white walls, dark grey wardrobe (white inside). Bed & bedsides are black.    Both black & off white will go well, but I can't decide between them for a Shanghai stand & Miami storage boxes for my wardrobe. I'm leaning towards black - the Shanghai will go under an aluminium steel mirror, & an aluminium steel light. Black will stand out so nicely against these. Here's an old photo of the unfinished room but it might help?     If I needed a stand downstairs, the off white would be a no-brainer against our Venetian plastered antique white spiral staircase. I could go with off white Miami's in my wardrobe to blend in with the white inside, but the perfectionist in me wants both products to be the same colour  🤓   Sorry for my ramblings - a small window into my indecisive brain! I'd value your thoughts? How does the off white cope with wear?    Lastly, I happen to be down with a migraine right now - this email has taken forever to finish! I hope to decide & place my order sometime today, but wanted to check how stock is for these items? I don't want to miss out!   Pooi Nie   On Thu, 27 Nov 2025 at 06:02, info < info@sagitine.com > wrote: Good Morning Pooi Nie,   Thank you so much for your message. Unfortunately, we’re not set up for click and collect, however, I’d love to offer you free shipping. Just enter FREESHIPBFCM when you checkout. And don’t forget that our sale ends soon, there are only a couple of days left to shop the discounts. If there’s anything else I can help you with, please don’t hesitate to reach out. subject: RE: Sydney Click & Collect?
customer_question: What's the status of my pre-order?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Oh that’s great news. Thank you so much for your order and your support, it really means the world.   The Shanghai Stand and Miaim Boxes should leave the warehouse early next week and be with you by late next week. The Insert Sets are both on pre-order with an estimated delivery of January.   Let me know if there’s anything else I can help you with. And I hope you have a great rest of the day. Warm Regards Heidi x   From: Pooi Nie Liew <pooinie@gmail.com> Sent: Thursday, 27 November 2025 12:36 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Sydney Click & Collect?   Heidi you are the best - thank you once again. I had the order lined up on my mobile phone so have just had to press PAY NOW. Am so excited!    And yes I totally agree there’s no need for extreme perfectionism across the whole home! 😎   What is the rough timing for delivery in Sydney? Pooi Nie     On Thu, 27 Nov 2025 at 11:53 am, info < info@sagitine.com > wrote: Oh Pooi Nie, I hope you’re okay.  Migraines are the absolute worst.   I’ll pop my advice in dot points so it’s easier to digest for you x   Availability: * Black Shanghai 14-Box Stand - In Stock * Off-White Shanghai 14-Box Stand - On pre-order with an estimated delivery of February. * Miami Boxes in Off-White and Black - In Stock   Wear: Both the Black and Off-White wear really well.  I have the Black that I’ve used pretty much daily for five years, and it still looks as good as new.    Styling Advice: TLDR; * Black Shanghai for bedroom * Black Miami for in cupboards * Off-White Shanghai for downstairs   The Black Shanghai would look amazing in your bedroom. Absolutely think that’s a great choice. It will work so well with the darkened pallet and will add to the drama but will be so chic.    I get the perfectionism!  Me too, so I would match the colour of the pieces in the room, so if you go with the Black Shanghai in your bedroom, do Black Miami’s for in your wardrobe.  But, I don’t think you need to match the colours across the house.  You could definitely do Off-White if you wanted to add a Stand downstairs.   I have the Black in my bedroom and then the Deep Blue in my office and am so happy that they match the environment they’re in rather than matching each other.   I hope that helped! Please let me know if there’s anything else I can with. And good luck with the migraine.  I hope you’re resting and it’s passed by the time you read this. Warm Regards Heidi x   From: Pooi Nie Liew < pooinie@gmail.com > Sent: Thursday, 27 November 2025 10:20 AM To: info < info@sagitine.com > Subject: Re: Sydney Click & Collect?   Oh gosh Heidi,   Thanks so much - I really appreciate your kindness!   Hoping for some help with my indecisiveness, if I could ask your opinion? My bedroom is quite dark (deliberately for my migraine condition): brown/black floorboards, antique white walls, dark grey wardrobe (white inside). Bed & bedsides are black.    Both black & off white will go well, but I can't decide between them for a Shanghai stand & Miami storage boxes for my wardrobe. I'm leaning towards black - the Shanghai will go under an aluminium steel mirror, & an aluminium steel light. Black will stand out so nicely against these. Here's an old photo of the unfinished room but it might help?     If I needed a stand downstairs, the off white would be a no-brainer against our Venetian plastered antique white spiral staircase. I could go with off white Miami's in my wardrobe to blend in with the white inside, but the perfectionist in me wants both products to be the same colour  🤓   Sorry for my ramblings - a small window into my indecisive brain! I'd value your thoughts? How does the off white cope with wear?    Lastly, I happen to be down with a migraine right now - this email has taken forever to finish! I hope to decide & place my order sometime today, but wanted to check how stock is for these items? I don't want to miss out!   Pooi Nie   On Thu, 27 Nov 2025 at 06:02, info < info@sagitine.com > wrote: Good Morning Pooi Nie,   Thank you so much for your message. subject: RE: Sydney showroom?
customer_question: What's the status of my pre-order?
response_body: Good Morning Jeraldine,   Thank you so much for your message, and for your interest in the Florence 8 in Off White, it’s such a lovely piece. Unfortunately, we sold out over the weekend.  They are one of our most popular products. We will be making them available again in November on pre-order with a current approximate delivery of January.  But I’m yet to firm up those details so they may change.   We don’t have a public showroom or offer warehouse visits, but I’d be very happy to send through additional photos and details to help you get a better sense of the stand and finish.  I have also included a few more details below.   The Florence 8 is one of our most versatile pieces, it’s the perfect height to use as a bedside or side table, adding both beauty and function to any space. It comes with eight of our Milan Boxes, which are made from premium paperboard. We’ve chosen this material because it’s strong, breathable, and kind to your clothing, while keeping the look beautifully refined.   The boxes aren’t fixed to the stand, so they slide in and out easily, just like storage racks, giving you flexible storage that’s as practical as it is elegant.   If there are any other specific details you’d like to see, or questions you have, please don’t hesitate to reach out.   I hope you have a wonderful week ahead.   Warm regards, Heidi x     From: Jeraldine <jeraldinemcmillan@gmail.com> Sent: Saturday, 4 October 2025 9:58 AM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Sydney showroom?   Good morning   I wondered if you are open to me visiting you in your Sydney warehouse to see a sample before purchase? I am in interested in the Florence 8 in offwhite.   Regards Jeraldine

response_body: Hi Suzanne,   Thank you so much for your message and for your interest in Sagitine.   We don’t currently have any Sydney window displays or showrooms available to view in person. That said, I’d be more than happy to help you feel confident before placing your order.   If there’s a particular piece or colour you’re considering, I can send through additional images, close-ups, or styling shots to help you get a better sense of the finish, scale, and how it looks in a real home setting. Just let me know which product you’re looking at, and I’ll take it from there.   Warm regards, Heidi x   From: S Riley <sriley.au@gmail.com> Sent: Tuesday, 30 December 2025 8:00 AM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Sydney Window Display Locations   Hi team,   Just wondering, are their any current  Sydney Window Display locations where I could go view your product before placing my order?   Thanks so much

source_filename: RE_ Tax Invoice for Sagitine Order #438239701 (588).msg
subject: RE: Tax Invoice for Sagitine Order #438239701
customer_question: How do I assemble/set up my Box?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Good Morning Carmen, Thank you so much for reaching out and I am so sorry to hear your Boxes aren’t uniform. Of course we’ll replace them.    I’ll replace all 14 of the Boxes to ensure there are no issues with the colour. Is it best to organise the replacement boxes to the same address? If you don’t mind, I’d love to take your current boxes back when we replace them with the new ones so I can show our product and manufacturing teams.  The Nougat is in our signature range and we have such a large percentage of repeat customers, we have several checks in place to ensure the boxes are a uniform colour for those purchasing a second or third time.   I’d love to be able to show the team the varying colours on the boxes so we can hopefully stop it from happening again. Thank you again for reaching out and my apologies once more for the issue. Warm Regards Heidi x   From: carmen cicchinelli <cmcicchinelli@gmail.com> Sent: Sunday, 6 April 2025 10:17 AM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Tax Invoice for Sagitine Order #438239701   Hi Heidi, I purchased this piece a few months ago and only recently assembled it. Upon inspection, I noticed that half of the drawers are a different colour than the others. Specifically, six of the drawers are a much warmer cream compared to the rest. The tags on the lighter drawers match their colour, while the tags on the other six are a different shade. I assume the drawers were both intended to be nougat, however it's clear they have been coloured in two different batches and are significantly different. Please see the image attached. Given the price of this piece, I would expect all the drawers to match in colour. I would appreciate it if the mismatched drawers could be replaced as soon as possible. Looking forward to hearing from you. Many

source_filename: RE_ Ten Zero Three Pty Ltd x ZhenHub Agreement (259).msg
subject: RE: Ten Zero Three Pty Ltd x ZhenHub Agreement
customer_question: How do I assemble this?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Hi Katherine, Thank you so much, I’ve just topped up the account.   The goods are actually ready a little earlier than expected, but there’s been a small mix-up. They were accidentally sent to the port for shipment to Australia, even though they were meant to come directly to you.   I’m just confirming which port they’ve been taken to now.   Once I have that, would you be able to quote on collecting the shipment from the port and transporting it to the warehouse? The total volume is approximately 40CBM. I’ll send the exact pick-up location and any required details as soon as I receive them.   I’m also getting an alternative quote in case it falls outside what you’re able to do, but I wanted to check with you first, as transporting the goods in bulk from the port to the warehouse may be something you can help with.   I’ll keep you updated the moment I have the port details.   Warm regards, Heidi       From: Katherine Famor <katherine@zhenhub.com> Sent: Wednesday, 10 December 2025 4:49 PM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com>; Account Update <account-update@zhenhub.com> Subject: RE: Ten Zero Three Pty Ltd x ZhenHub Agreement   Thanks Heidi,   Please inform me once top up done enable us to inform the accounts and on-boarding team. Best Regards,  Katherine --  ZhenHub  |  Signup Facebook  |  X  |  LinkedIn December 10 at 1:16 PM, Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> wrote: [EXTERNAL EMAIL]: DO NOT provide username and password under any circumstances. Confirm links and attachments are safe before clicking or opening. Hi Katherine, Apologies for missing those documents. Please find attached the Ten Zero Three ASIC Certificate and also a utility bill verifying my address. I’ll take a look at the top up video below and let you know once I’ve deposited $1,000 USD. Thank you again for all of your help. Warm Regards Heidi   From: Katherine Famor <katherine@zhenhub.com> Sent: Wednesday, 10 December 2025 12:40 PM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com>; Account Update <account-update@zhenhub.com> Subject: RE: Ten Zero Three Pty Ltd x ZhenHub Agreement   Hi Heidi,   Thank you for completing the form and can you  please provide your business registration certificate.   For proof of address, you may submit either a recent utility bill or a national ID that includes your current address.   For the top up - please see attach guide   Thank you and have a good day ahead!   Best Regards,  Katherine --  ZhenHub  |  Signup Facebook  |  X  |  LinkedIn   December 10 at 8:58 AM, Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> wrote: [EXTERNAL EMAIL]: DO NOT provide username and password under any circumstances. Confirm links and attachments are safe before clicking or opening. Hello ZhenHub Legal team, Thank you so much for the agreement. Please find attached the signed, initialled and dated agreement.   Can you please resend information on how to make the deposit? The link supplied isn’t working for me. Thank you again and look forward to hearing back from you. Have a wonderful day Warm Regards Heidi   From: ZhenHub Legal <account-update@zhenhub.com> Sent: Thursday, 4 December 2025 1:24 PM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: Ten Zero Three Pty Ltd x ZhenHub Agreement   Dear Ms Heidi Melville , Please see the attached ZhenHub agreements. Please carefully read through the agreements and let us know if you have any questions. If it is clear, please sign the below agreements and return: 1. MSA (sign page 9) 2. Fulfillment Agreement (sign page 25) 3. Company Registration Certificate or equivalent 4. Proof of Address (eg. Utility Bill, Phone Bill etc. within the last 3 months) 5. Make your USD1000 deposit (instructions on how to do this: https://bit.ly/2U4SNBZ ) Remember to initial each page as well AND email the signed copy of your agreement to your Onboarding Specialist or Account Executive. The next steps will be organizing your shipment to the warehouse and scheduling a system training with your System Onboarding Specialist. Regards, ZhenHub Legal Team Sent via Document Studio

subject: RE: The Mothering Project Q&A
customer_question: I just wanted to share my feedback
response_body: Oh jeez Nic, I am so so sorry for the lateness, but more for the radio silence. I feel really rude.   But!  The questions are done.     It was actually a really lovely process to go through and think back on those early days and reflect more deeply on being a parent than I have been before. There were a few questions that really brought up a lot of feelings. Thank you for that, it was a really lovely reflection.   Please feel free to edit to your hearts content. Honestly, no preciousness at all about changing, altering, rescripting, editing anything at all.   Thanks so much again Nics. And my apologies once more for the lateness. Heidi x   From: Nic Redfern <nicolerredfern@gmail.com> Sent: Thursday, 5 March 2026 3:15 PM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: The Mothering Project Q&A   Hey Heidi,    Hope you're having a good week!    Just checking in on this, let me know if you have any questions and when might suit you to send through your answers.

subject: RE: Tidio [1 new message] Tanelle  (tanellejs@live.com.au)
customer_question: What's the status of my pre-order?

subject: RE: Tips
customer_question: How do I assemble this?
response_body: Hi Andrea, Thank you so much for your message, I’m so happy to help. Our Boxes are designed with a purposefully snug fit, which limits household dust getting inside. Because of this, they don’t behave like everyday drawers with runners or mechanics. A few tips that usually help: Use two hands when opening, one on each side of the front panel rather than pulling from the centre. Slide slowly and evenly, keeping the Box level as you open it. Many customers also find it easiest to lift the Box out of the Stand entirely when accessing shoes more frequently. After a short period of regular use, the fit typically relaxes slightly and becomes easier to handle. If it still feels unusually tight after trying the above, please let me know and I’m more than happy to look into it further for you. Warm regards, Heidi x -----Original Message----- From: Andrea Henderson <edwar07@live.com> Sent: Sunday, 18 January 2026 1:59 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Tips Hi, Any tips to make the drawer slide out of the box easier. Mine is tight for help of them. Regards Andrea

subject: RE: Trade Account
customer_question: How do I return this?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Good Morning Rose,   You are so welcome.   Our returns policy is pretty flexible, if you or a client aren’t happy with your purchase just let us know as soon as you can and we’ll organise a return and a refund.  You’ll need to make sure you keep any packaging and send it back in its original packaging at your cost. Once we receive the items back we’ll issue the refund.   We’re an online only business so unfortunately we don’t have any stockists in Melbourne, but if you let me know which pieces you’re considering I’m more than happy to send you through some additional images.   Let me know if that would help. Warm Regards Heidi x     From: Rose Boland <rose@roseboland.com> Sent: Wednesday, 28 May 2025 3:48 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Trade Account   Thank you SO MUCH!   What is your return policy on items bought at discounted rate?   Do you have a showroom or any stockists I can view pieces, in Melbourne?

source_filename: RE_ Trade Quote Request - Clovelly Project .msg
subject: RE: Trade Quote Request - Clovelly Project
customer_question: Do you offer wholesale pricing?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Good Morning Alivia, Thank you so much for reaching out, I hope you’re having a great week. We offer trade pricing at 15% off the RRP plus shipping. Therefore, the trade price for the following pieces would be:   2 x Shanghai 14 Box Stands in Deep Blue: $2,805 Shipping: $50 Total: $2,855   The way we facilitate trade orders is to ask for a few details like ABN and contact information. I then set you up with a discount code that you’re able to use whenever you have an order come through. We can then ship the products to you or directly to your customer, depending on which delivery address you use when you place the order. And the invoicing information comes through to whichever email address you use when you place the order. We do have a consumer offer on at the moment for 20% off during our Warehouse Sale, however, that promotion ends at midnight tonight. If you were looking to place your trade order next week though, I’d be more than happy to extend the 20% off to you until midnight on Wednesday 7 th of May.   I hope that helps and please let me know if there’s anything else you need. Warm Regards Heidi x     From: Alivia Pagano / Lynne Bradley Interiors <admin@lynnebradleyinteriors.com.au> Sent: Tuesday, 29 April 2025 11:01 AM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Trade Quote Request - Clovelly Project   Hello,    Could we please get a trade quote for the below:    * 2 x Shanghai 14 Box Stand * Colour: Deep Blue * Stand: black  * Pulls: navy

source_filename: RE_ We need your help_ please! (53).msg
subject: RE: We need your help, please!
customer_question: How do I assemble/set up my Box?
response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Good Afternoon Ben,   Thank you so much for your message, and I’m so sorry to hear there has been sickness in the family. I hope everyone is feeling much better now.   I truly appreciate you taking such care during assembly and thank you for explaining the situation so clearly. I completely understand wanting the top surface to be in beautiful, mint condition, especially as it’s the most visible part of the Stand.  I feel the same way.   Of course, we will replace the Stand for you.  Are you happy for me to arrange delivery to the same address we delivered the original order to?   Rather than collecting the current Stand, we have a small product stewardship approach here at Sagitine where we try to minimise unnecessary transport and waste wherever possible. If you’re able to reuse or reimagine the piece in another space, perhaps in a wardrobe, storage room, or even as a utility shelf, we would absolutely love for it to have a second life.  If that’s not suitable for you, please let me know. I’m more than happy to look into connecting with a local artist or creative reuse initiative who may be able to collect it and repurpose it thoughtfully.   Thank you again for your patience and for your kind words about the piece. It really does mean so much. We’ll make sure you have a flawless Stand to enjoy very soon.   Warm regards, Heidi x     From: Ben Schuhmacher <b.schuhmacher85@hotmail.com> Sent: Thursday, 19 February 2026 10:59 AM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: We need your help, please!   Dear Heidi, We received the beautiful Sagitine stand and boxes last week — thank you.  Unfortunately, there was some sickness in the family, so it took us a number of days before we were able to carefully unpack and assemble everything. Once assembled, we noticed there are visible scratches on the top of the stand. I just want to mention that we were very careful during assembly and put it together on top of a towel, so the scratches were not caused during that process. Had the marks been on the bottom or the back, we would have happily overlooked them. However, as they are on the top, they are noticeable. We absolutely love the piece and were really hoping to keep it in mint condition. For that reason, we were wondering if it might be possible to arrange a swap for a replacement stand? Please let us know how best to proceed or if you have an alternative suggestion. I've attached photos of the scratched areas. Thank you in advance for your help - we appreciate it and look forward to resolving this.

customer_question: Where is my order?
response_body: Good Morning Natalie, Thank you so much for letting me know. We’ll ask them for proof of delivery and find out where it was delivered to. I hope you have a lovely day ahead. Warm Regards Heidi   From: Natalie Engel <natalie.engel24@gmail.com> Sent: Monday, 1 September 2025 3:08 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Hello Heidi,   Thankyou for you email and following up on multiple occasions.   Could you ask Sendle where their local delivery partner delivered the parcel too, because I have not seen it as yet.   Regards, Natalie    On 1 Sep 2025, at 10:41 am, info < info@sagitine.com > wrote: ﻿ Good Morning Natalie, I hope you’re well. We’ve been notified by Sendle that you have received two of the three packages for your order. Exciting to get some progress, but very frustrating the entire order wasn’t delivered at once. We’ve reached out to them this morning to follow up on where the third box is and when it’s due to arrive. In the meantime, I’ve reattached the assembly instructions for you for ease. I’ll be back in touch before the end of the day with any updates from Sendle. Warm Regards Heidi     From: info < info@sagitine.com > Sent: Saturday, 30 August 2025 6:11 PM To: info < info@sagitine.com >; Natalie Engel < natalie.engel24@gmail.com > Subject: RE: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Good Evening Natalie, I hope you’re having a lovely weekend. Apologies for not getting back to your yesterday afternoon, both Stew and I have been following up with Sendle non stop and I was very much hoping to have an estimated arrival date. We’ve been advised that your order is with their local delivery partner but they won’t give us the contact details for that person.   We will continue to follow up and be back in touch on Monday with an update. I hope you have a wonderful rest of the weekend. Warm Regards Heidi   From: info < info@sagitine.com > Sent: Thursday, 28 August 2025 4:48 PM To: info < info@sagitine.com >; Natalie Engel < natalie.engel24@gmail.com > Subject: RE: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Good Afternoon Natalie, I hope you’ve had a nice day.   I wanted to email back as promised with an update, however, unfortunately we are still yet to receive any news on the delivery status of your order.   As you can imagine, we have been following up with Sendle all day. We will continue to follow up with them tomorrow and be back in touch with you before the weekend.   Warm Regards Heidi       From: info < info@sagitine.com > Sent: Thursday, 28 August 2025 11:47 AM To: Natalie Engel < natalie.engel24@gmail.com >; info < info@sagitine.com > Subject: RE: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Good Morning Natalie, I am so so sorry for the delay with your order. I am seeing your emails and did ask Stew to follow up as he is best placed to get to the bottom of the issue with Sendle. I can only imagine how incredibly frustrating this is for you. Stew has a contact at Sendle that he’s working with to get your order delivered as our top delivery priority.   The complication that he’s uncovered is that Sendle sometimes pass their regional deliveries onto third party delivery partners.  Therefore, their tracking updates and communication can be delayed once the packages has been processed in your local area.   We are waiting on both Stew’s contact and a highlighted investigation via Sendle for an update on when your order will be delivered. We will be in touch as soon as we hear anything from Sendle.  And regardless, I’ll get back in touch with you this afternoon to give you an update.   In the meantime, I have also double checked our noted delivery times on our website and have updated some of the wording so it is even clearer that delivery to regional areas can sometimes take between 4 to 6 weeks to arrive.   My sincerest apologies again Natalie. Delivery is such a challenging part of business in Australia, as you put it previously, our vastness means for a high level of unreliability.   I hope the rest of your day is lovely and I’ll be back in touch this afternoon. Warm Regards Heidi       From: Natalie Engel < natalie.engel24@gmail.com > Sent: Thursday, 28 August 2025 8:29 AM To: info < info@sagitine.com > Subject: Re: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Hello again Heidi,   This is another email requesting me to review a product which has, wait for it ….not arrived!  Shocking, I know right!!   And just to add to this saga, Sendle have lost the order and are requesting another full week to conduct an investigation on what happened to the order.   This is starting to sound very suspicious. I have had issues with couriers stealing goods before.    If it was me, I would be putting a bomb under someone in Management at Sendle, because if this is the way they handle your deliveries, it affects your business reputation.    You haven’t replied to my previous email however I know you are reading them as suddenly Stewart follows up with an email, regarding updates.    Regards,   Natalie    On 27 Aug 2025, at 12:14 pm, Heidi, Sagitine Owner < info@sagitine.com > wrote: ﻿ Hi Natalie, I thought I'd send one more email to see if you'd be open to sharing your thoughts on Sagitine. I know it'll take years to grow a business like Sagitine, but a few seconds of your time will go a long way in helping us thrive. Your feedback tells me what we're doing right, where we can improve and helps us to create the best products for you and our community.  I hope Sagitine continues to warm your home. Heidi xx   P.S. Don't forget to use FREESHIP if you would like to add another piece to your collection. You received this email from Sagitine. If you would like to unsubscribe, click here . <image001.jpg> <SAGITINE - Shanghai 14 Box Stand - Assembly Instructions.pdf>

Warm Regards Heidi   From: info < info@sagitine.com > Sent: Thursday, 28 August 2025 4:48 PM To: info < info@sagitine.com >; Natalie Engel < natalie.engel24@gmail.com > Subject: RE: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Good Afternoon Natalie, I hope you’ve had a nice day.   I wanted to email back as promised with an update, however, unfortunately we are still yet to receive any news on the delivery status of your order.   As you can imagine, we have been following up with Sendle all day. We will continue to follow up with them tomorrow and be back in touch with you before the weekend.   Warm Regards Heidi       From: info < info@sagitine.com > Sent: Thursday, 28 August 2025 11:47 AM To: Natalie Engel < natalie.engel24@gmail.com >; info < info@sagitine.com > Subject: RE: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Good Morning Natalie, I am so so sorry for the delay with your order. I am seeing your emails and did ask Stew to follow up as he is best placed to get to the bottom of the issue with Sendle. I can only imagine how incredibly frustrating this is for you. Stew has a contact at Sendle that he’s working with to get your order delivered as our top delivery priority.   The complication that he’s uncovered is that Sendle sometimes pass their regional deliveries onto third party delivery partners.  Therefore, their tracking updates and communication can be delayed once the packages has been processed in your local area.   We are waiting on both Stew’s contact and a highlighted investigation via Sendle for an update on when your order will be delivered. We will be in touch as soon as we hear anything from Sendle.  And regardless, I’ll get back in touch with you this afternoon to give you an update.   In the meantime, I have also double checked our noted delivery times on our website and have updated some of the wording so it is even clearer that delivery to regional areas can sometimes take between 4 to 6 weeks to arrive.   My sincerest apologies again Natalie. Delivery is such a challenging part of business in Australia, as you put it previously, our vastness means for a high level of unreliability.   I hope the rest of your day is lovely and I’ll be back in touch this afternoon. Warm Regards Heidi       From: Natalie Engel < natalie.engel24@gmail.com > Sent: Thursday, 28 August 2025 8:29 AM To: info < info@sagitine.com > Subject: Re: We'd really appreciate your thoughts, Natalie – it only takes a few seconds   Hello again Heidi,   This is another email requesting me to review a product which has, wait for it ….not arrived!  Shocking, I know right!!   And just to add to this saga, Sendle have lost the order and are requesting another full week to conduct an investigation on what happened to the order.   This is starting to sound very suspicious. I have had issues with couriers stealing goods before.    If it was me, I would be putting a bomb under someone in Management at Sendle, because if this is the way they handle your deliveries, it affects your business reputation.    You haven’t replied to my previous email however I know you are reading them as suddenly Stewart follows up with an email, regarding updates.    Regards,   Natalie    On 27 Aug 2025, at 12:14 pm, Heidi, Sagitine Owner < info@sagitine.com > wrote: ﻿ Hi Natalie, I thought I'd send one more email to see if you'd be open to sharing your thoughts on Sagitine. Would you mind if I asked what size shoe you are and what type of shoes you’re looking to fit in the boxes so I can take that into consideration if we look to change the Box size or ever introduce another size of Box?   I’m so happy you love the product in general though. To say thank you for taking the time for the feedback please accept this free shipping code for any future orders you place this year.  The code is FREESHIP , just enter it at checkout.   Also, your review is really really helpful for other people with yours or a similar size shoe that are thinking of purchasing the Stand, so if you would like to, I’d encourage you to leave a public review on for the Stand HERE so help our other customers.   Thank you again for your feedback and taking the time to let me know. I hope you have a wonderful rest of the week. response_body: v\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} o\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} w\:* {behavior:url(#default#VML);} .shape {behavior:url(#default#VML);} Good Morning Steve,   Thank you so much for your message, I’m so happy to help.   I’ve double-checked our system and can confirm that no orders were successfully placed under your name, which aligns with what you saw when the payment was taken and then automatically refunded.   This can sometimes happen if the billing address linked to your card doesn’t exactly match the delivery address entered at checkout, which causes our payment system to decline the transaction as a security measure.   To make this as easy as possible, I’ve just sent through a draft order to your email address with the 10% discount already applied. You’ll receive this separately, simply follow the links in that email to complete checkout.   Please double check the order and delivery address are correct and that the email is the best for updates on your pre-order arrival. Just a quick note to reiterate that the Shanghai 14 Box Stands in Off White are currently on pre-order, with an expected arrival of mid to late February.   If you have any questions at all or need a hand completing the order, please don’t hesitate to reply here, I’m more than happy to help.   Warm regards, Heidi x   From: Steve Zammit <stevemzammit@gmail.com> Sent: Thursday, 22 January 2026 1:39 PM To: info <info@sagitine.com> Subject: Re: Welcome to Sagitine   Hi Sagitine Team,   I was hoping you could assist. I had issues processing my order last night of  2x  Shanghai 14 Box Stand - Off White. I was using the 10% code WELCOME10 that I received.   The payment was taken then refunded.    Can you please assist me to complete the order.   Thanks   Steve 0420597176       On Wed, Jan 21, 2026 at 9:51 PM Sagitine < info@sagitine.com > wrote: Organise with elegance, preserve with style ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­   T&Cs. 10% off offer applies to your first order. Heidi x   From: Kami Dougherty <kami@nextpack.com.au> Sent: Tuesday, 27 January 2026 11:28 AM To: Heidi Melville <heidi@sagitine.com> Subject: RE: Welcome to Zhenhub, Heidi!   HI Heidi,   It was nice long weekend. Kids back to school this week and the rat race begins.   The ETD for the China delivery is 29 th Jan. They needed to print all the carton markings and pack.   As for the trucking of your stands we could organise for you. The stand is with your other supplier correct? Have they been able to advise you on transporting the stands or did they say you need to organise yourself?   Warm regards,   Kami Dougherty Head of Operations Nextpack Pty Ltd       M 0429 499 025 E    kami@nextpack.com.au W www.nextpack.com.au A   Suite 302, 115 Military Rd Neutral Bay NSW 2089 Australia P  PO BOX 753 Neutral Bay NSW 2089 Australia       From: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Sent: Tuesday, 27 January 2026 10:35 AM To: Kami Dougherty < kami@nextpack.com.au > Subject: RE: Welcome to Zhenhub, Heidi!   Hi Kami, Hope you’re great. Are you please able to let me know if there are tracking details and when the shipment should arrive so I can update the portal? And also, are you please able to let me know who you used to send the goods?  I’m organising for the Stand order to go there too. Hope you had a fabulous long weekend! Heidi x   From: Kami Dougherty < kami@nextpack.com.au > Sent: Wednesday, 21 January 2026 11:08 AM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Subject: RE: Welcome to Zhenhub, Heidi!   HI Heidi,   For trucking and packing and loading the China portion of the order the cost is AUD2,765. This quote is lower because we are not palletising.   Per our conversation, please confirm you agree.   Thanks.   Warm regards,   Kami Dougherty Head of Operations Nextpack Pty Ltd       M 0429 499 025 E    kami@nextpack.com.au W www.nextpack.com.au A   Suite 302, 115 Military Rd Neutral Bay NSW 2089 Australia P  PO BOX 753 Neutral Bay NSW 2089 Australia       From: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Sent: Saturday, 17 January 2026 1:30 PM To: Kami Dougherty < kami@nextpack.com.au > Subject: RE: Welcome to Zhenhub, Heidi!   Hi Kami, Thanks so much for the info.  And yup, the warehouse is climate controlled. Thanks again though for checking in. Here’s the PDF again. Let me know if it doesn’t work and I’ll redownload it.   From: Kami Dougherty < kami@nextpack.com.au > Sent: Friday, 16 January 2026 10:45 AM To: Heidi Melville < heidi@sagitine.com > Subject: RE: Welcome to Zhenhub, Heidi!   HI Heidi,   For some reason I cannot open that file. Could you resend it.   Is Zhen Hub climate controlled? I don’t know how long you will keep the boxes there and I wanted to make you aware of the monsoon season in China is from Feb to June. This is just the beginning of an exciting journey to grow your business with us. To help you make the most of what ZhenHub offers, our Customer Success team is here to guide you through onboarding, system training, and connecting with our network of fulfillment centers. They bring a wealth of knowledge and insights to help you fully leverage our platform to scale your operations.   Attached is a copy of our  Product Label Guide  for your reference. Feel free to bring up any questions during your first system training session.   Let me introduce you to your  System Training Specialist, Lyra . She will be your main point of contact to ensure smooth operations, manage shipments, and address any concerns quickly, so you can focus on growing your business with confidence.   Lyra and the team are already up to speed with your account. I’ve shared your contract and account details. If you have any additional requests or requirements that haven’t been mentioned yet, please don’t hesitate to let her know.   I hope your onboarding experience has been smooth so far. You’re in great hands, and we’re excited to support your journey with ZhenHub.   Thank you!   Best Regards,  Katherine --  ZhenHub  |  Signup Facebook  |  X  |  LinkedIn
